title: Rhynchospora diandra
taxonomy: Rhynchospora diandra is a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. It belongs to the genus Rhynchospora and is also known as the lesser club-sedge. As a member of the family Cyperaceae, Rhynchospora diandra is a type of monocotyledonous plant. The genus Rhynchospora is part of the order Poales. This classification places Rhynchospora diandra within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms.
uses: Rhynchospora diandra is often used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which may include treating fever and other infections. Some studies suggest that the plant's extracts may possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots and rhizomes of Rhynchospora diandra are typically used in herbal remedies. In some cultures, the plant is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Rhynchospora diandra's medicinal uses.

Its extracts have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antifungal, and antibacterial activities.
